subject: Mira Tan
subject_note: FICTIONAL figure — a veteran product-design mentor invented for this illustration; the corpus below does not exist and characterizes no real person.
focus: design-judgment lens only — how she critiques and simplifies product flows (not org-building / hiring views)
intended_use: thinking advisor — a design-crit lens the studio can run on a pasted flow

mode.corpus_mode_reason: Phase 1 mode table: subject is a fictional non-public figure, so no web search is run; the six lanes mine only the supplied invented corpus.

research_plan.research_1_writings: Notes on Friction essay series (~80 essays) — repeated core claims (>=3x), coined terms, reading list
research_plan.research_2_conversations: The Second Draft podcast (~60 teardown episodes) — answers under follow-up, improvised analogies, position shifts
research_plan.research_3_expression_dna: 'Mira's margins' short posts — sentence fingerprint, signature vocabulary, taboo words, humor, certainty register
research_plan.research_4_external_views: studio write-ups + conference recaps — observed patterns, real criticisms, peer contrast
research_plan.research_5_decisions: three narrated case studies — actual logic, hindsight, say/do consistency
research_plan.research_6_timeline: invented career timeline — milestones, inflection points, last 12 months

rules: Capture HOW she thinks, not WHAT she said — frameworks over quotes.
Keep contradictions; do not smooth them — her tensions are the signal.
Honest 60 over fake 90 — flag gaps rather than fabricate.
Long-form > soundbites; controversy > consensus; change > constancy.
honest_boundary_seed: This skill is a lens, not Mira; it cannot predict her on a genuinely new problem.
Public voice may diverge from private view.
Information is fictional and frozen at the (invented) research date.
source: intake framing per huashu-nuwa Phase 0A 需求澄清 + the 核心理念 HOW-not-WHAT distinction
